item.title: World Cup: Racist commentary called out in media coverage, hurting diversity at the top
item.description: Leading experts say racial stereotypes in World Cup coverage can be damaging for athletes' leadership aspirations and have an impact on Black communities more broadly.

item.title: Adelaide automotive dismantler penalised $25k for not giving adequate meal breaks
item.description: An automotive industry group made threats "to create fear" in a man who had brought legal action against his employer for not giving him adequate lunch breaks, a tribunal judgement has found.

item.title: Fears for jobs as SunRice cuts shifts due to drought and water buybacks
item.description: Communities in southern NSW fear large cutbacks to rice processing could put hundreds of people out of work and decimate regional economies.

item.title: Police investigate two suspicious house fires that started minutes apart in neighbouring Melbourne suburbs
item.description: The two house fires started within minutes of each other, less than two kilometres apart in Melbourne's north.

item.title: NT Aboriginal language speakers call for stronger language revival efforts
item.description: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

item.title: In clash of AFL titans Fremantle and Sydney, who will emerge as premiership favourites?
item.description: Fremantle take on Sydney at Perth Stadium tonight, in a match boasting storylines as compelling as early-era Melrose Place. Who will emerge from the clash as premiership favourites?

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: British wildcard Arthur Fery sets up semifinal clash with Alexander Zverev
item.description: After growing up just five minutes from Wimbledon, Arthur Fery is now through to the semifinals at his home grand slam.

item.title: Union raised safety concerns with freight train operator day before Berrima derailment
item.description: The rail union says it raised safety concerns with a freight train operator about 24 hours before a major derailment in the New South Wales Southern Highlands.

item.title: Scammers use AI image to target parents of WA missing man
item.description: Scammers have sent the parents of a missing West Australian man an AI-generated image of their son and demanded thousands of dollars in exchange for his safety.

item.title: NSW coach Laurie Daley slams 'hidden agendas' after State of Origin triumph
item.description: Laurie Daley has the last laugh over critics who hounded him ahead of the State of Origin decider at Lang Park.

item.title: Researchers harvest water from the air to grow native seedlings and rehydrate the Wheatbelt
item.description: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

item.title: Telstra outage: 'Software defect solution' in place after hundreds unable to call Triple Zero in outage
item.description: Telstra has implemented a fresh "solution" to resolve a secondary software defect that left hundreds of customers unable to make Triple Zero calls during a nationwide network outage.

item.title: Ukraine's Kostyuk extends Wimbledon run and slams IOC decision on Russia
item.description: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk is through to the Wimbledon semifinals. The 24-year-old says she finds it "tough" as her hometown of Kyiv continues to come under attack from Russia.

item.title: Albury families concerned over axing of local NDIS-funded program
item.description: An Albury-based NDIS provider says an upcoming change to NDIS funding is one of the reasons it will discontinue a program that builds skills and confidence in those living with a disability.

item.title: Queensland police check on 21 callers after national Telstra outage
item.description: Queensland police confirmed multiple emergency calls across the state failed to connect to Triple Zero during the nationwide Telstra outage.

item.title: Emmys 2026: Widow's Bay sneaks in, Rob Reiner honoured and more Emmy surprises
item.description: Plus Connor Storrie nabs his first nom but not for Heated Rivalry, Hacks soars on a swan song and more successes, surprises and snubs from today's Emmy nominations.

item.title: The Desert Champions Way: Outback Camel Trail, 2026, starts with camel races in Jundah
item.description: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.

item.title: WA gold project Warrawoona fined $118,000 over threatened bat
item.description: A Western Australian gold miner has been hit with a fine of more than $100,000 for failing to protect a vulnerable bat species.

item.title: Search for alternatives to paraquat continues after usage restricted
item.description: Australia buys most of its paraquat from China, but is investigating ways to become less reliant on the chemical.

item.title: US judge says E Jean Carroll can receive $8 million payment in Trump sexual abuse case
item.description: A Manhattan judge orders the payment to the former Elle magazine advice columnist, who a jury found was sexually abused by Donald Trump.

item.title: FIFA president Gianni Infantino referred to Olympics ethics investigators over Donald Trump's World Cup intervention
item.description: Gianni Infantino's close ties with Donald Trump continue to land him in hot water after the US President's World Cup intervention.

item.title: Democrats' Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner suspends midterm campaign after sexual assault accusation
item.description: The Democratic Senate candidate in the US state of Maine has suspended his midterm election campaign after a string of controversies, including an accusation of sexual assault.

item.title: Neil the seal 'may have moved on', Tasmanian wildlife experts say
item.description: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

item.title: US strikes Iran and reinstates oil sanctions, but Tehran has already raked in billions
item.description: The ceasefire between the United States and Iran is fraying and Washington has attempted to crush the regime's oil sales, but Tehran made its move when the blockade lifted weeks ago.

item.title: I'd always wanted to carry a baby. Cancer meant I couldn't
item.description: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

item.title: Trainee surgeon Ryan Cho pleads guilty to secretly filming hundreds of victims
item.description: Ryan Cho has pleaded guilty to 13 charges, including stalking, producing an intimate image and installing an optical surveillance device.

item.title: Life returns to streets around damaged NYC high-rise
item.description: A Midtown Manhattan high-rise being converted into apartments has been stabilised after buckling support columns triggered evacuations and fears of a collapse.

item.title: How Hudson Young and Liam Martin bashed their way to State of Origin glory
item.description: Liam Martin and Hudson Young don't like to point fingers, unless it comes time to decide which of the New South Wales backrowers is madder.

item.title: Fremantle's quality talls and settled defence key as Dockers chase first-ever AFL flag
item.description: Fremantle has lost its 14-game winning streak with a loss to GWS, but what are the keys for the Dockers to keep going and set up a tilt at their first flag? Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson investigate.

item.title: Egypt demands FIFA investigate referees after controversial World Cup loss to Argentina
item.description: Egypt remains incensed at French referee Francois Letexier after his performance in the Round of 16 clash against Argentina at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Swimming Australia to take proactive, world-first approach to 'scary' fake news stories
item.description: In a world-first initiative, Swimming Australia is taking a proactive approach to deal with clickbait and fake news headlines about its athletes, including Olympic champion Mollie O'Callaghan, who has been targeted in the past.

item.title: Search continues after Boeing cargo plane crashes off coast of Pakistan
item.description: Rescue crews battle rough sea conditions to recover parts of a Boeing cargo plane that crashed into the Arabian Sea. Five crew members remain missing.

item.title: Telstra outage leaves elderly woman with dementia 'frightened', as senator's comments labelled 'irresponsible'
item.description: An SA woman says her elderly mother was left "frightened" during the Telstra outage after a fall. Meanwhile, SA police have said they have no record of any deaths during the network failure, despite a senator's claims.

item.title: Maryborough teenager allegedly told police he had planned mass murder for months
item.description: According to court documents, the 13-year-old told police he had planned mass murder for many months, had been inspired by other school shooters and massacres.

item.title: Sixteen-year-old boy charged with murder after stabbing in Taree
item.description: Police say a 16-year-old boy accused of murdering a 36-year-old man on the NSW Mid North Coast was known to the deceased.

item.title: Gout Gout ruled out of World Under-20 athletics titles after training injury
item.description: Australian athletics star Gout Gout is ruled out of this year's World Under-20 titles in Oregon after a training injury in Brisbane.

item.title: Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli gets plum WA cabinet spot after Paul Papalia resignation
item.description: New Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli, the former chief of staff to both Mark McGowan and Roger Cook, will be parachuted into cabinet to replace Paul Papalia, the ABC understands.

item.title: NT Police charge 34yo Jordan Garrawurra with murder after 17yo girl found dead in Galiwin'ku home
item.description: Days after a 17-year-old girl was found dead inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, police have charged a 34-year-old man following an autopsy.

item.title: Man who fatally stabbed housemate found not criminally responsible
item.description: The Supreme Court has returned a "special verdict" in the case of a man who fatally stabbed his 61-year-old housemate with a kitchen knife.

item.title: Court told former Wagga Wagga MP Daryl Maguire 'kept in dark' over fraudulent visa scheme
item.description: Daryl Maguire's barrister has told a jury it was "unbelievable" to think the ex NSW MP would recruit people in his electorate to take part in an allegedly fraudulent visa scheme.

item.title: Telstra outage estimated to cost hundreds of millions, but compensation not guaranteed
item.description: The economic cost of the Telstra network outage could run into hundreds of millions of dollars, an expert estimates.

item.title: State of Origin Game III the highest-rating program in series history
item.description: An unprecedented number of viewers tuned into the State of Origin decider, in which NSW stunned Queensland to win the series.

item.title: Death investigated by SA Police after senator linked it to Telstra outage
item.description: Police in SA are investigating a death at a regional South Australian hospital yesterday, after Liberal Senator Kerrynne Liddle said an elderly person had died during the Telstra outage.

item.title: From TikTok to podcasts, politics is chasing attention but there's a downside
item.description: Every press conference, reel and podcast is now a chance for politicians to connect with voters. Yet the strategy that broadens an MP's reach can also amplify their biggest mistakes.

item.title: NSW Police officer taken into custody after sexually assaulting, choking then-wife
item.description: A NSW Police officer has been taken into custody after his bail was revoked by a judge, two days after he was found guilty of choking and sexually assaulting his then-wife.

item.title: Cairns man who dissolved wife's body in wheelie bin of acid loses appeal bid for earlier release
item.description: Klaus Andres was sentenced to life in prison in 2013 after he was found guilty of murdering his 42-year-old wife Li Ping Cao.

item.title: Angus Taylor says One Nation policies would bring 'eternity of pain' to economy
item.description: In his strongest remarks pushing back on One Nation, the opposition leader has accused the right-wing party of being a "column of smoke".

item.title: Canberra Hospital opens Australia's first centre dedicated to treating complex immune diseases
item.description: The Centre for Personalised Medicine at Canberra Hospital uses cutting-edge technology to identify the causes of complex immune conditions to tailor treatments for patients.

item.title: ICAC told Alyssa White celebrated jobs for friends while UOW was 'broke'
item.description: The ICAC inquiry heard Alyssa White had celebrated securing jobs for former colleagues as the "greatest achievement" of her career before agreeing recruitment processes were compromised, lacked transparency and breached university policies.

item.title: NAIDOC celebrates strength of Warumungu culture with gallery opening in Tennant Creek
item.description: Hundreds of people walked through the streets of Tennant Creek today as part of a NAIDOC march, finishing at the official reopening of the town's major First Nations cultural hub.

item.title: Calls to invest in support services as Tasmania Police review finds pressures mounting
item.description: A review of Tasmania Police finds calls for help have risen by 40,000 annually over the past decade, fuelling demands for investment in early intervention support services.

item.title: Thursday Finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Family pleads for information about 'gorgeous' missing Toowoomba mum
item.description: Police are searching for a 30-year-old woman who was last seen in Toowoomba on Tuesday.

item.title: 'Why do it?' Coaches lament taunts in Fremantle's furious comeback win
item.description: Luke Ryan and Dane Rampe are both penalised for taunting opponents in Fremantle's win over Sydney, leaving both their coaches unimpressed.

item.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of power ballad Total Eclipse of the Heart, dies at 75
item.description: Welsh pop singer Bonnie Tyler, ​famed for 1980s power ballads such as Total Eclipse of the ​Heart and Holding Out ​for a Hero, has died.

item.title: Three NT corrections officers stood down with pay over prisoner escape
item.description: The Northern Territory Corrections Department has stood down three of its workers with pay over a prisoner escape on Sunday that triggered a more than 48-hour manhunt.

item.title: Indian Prime Minister’s visit met with both enthusiasm and protests
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Narendra Modi given rapturous welcome as he addresses thousands at community event in Melbourne
item.description: More than 20,000 people have given a rapturous welcome to Narendra Modi at Melbourne's Docklands Stadium as Anthony Albanese heaps praise on Australia's Indian diaspora.

item.title: World leaders gifted vintage revolvers by Erdogan after NATO summit
item.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said the gifts were intended as a showcase of his nation's defence industry.

item.title: Iran targets US military sites in Gulf as Israel warns it could 'strike again'
item.description: Iranian armed forces launched reciprocal attacks on US military infrastructure in Gulf states following US strikes on Iran's south and east regions, straining a three-week-old ceasefire.

item.title: Hiatus Kaiyote celebrate 15 years as rule-breaking favourites of Beyoncé, Drake, Prince
item.description: They've made admirers out of music's biggest names over their 15-year career, but this Australian group are always thinking about the future.

item.title: ABC weekly news quiz: Red cards, ballistic missiles and Pam the Bird
item.description: It's been a week of missile testing, Telstra outages and FIFA World Cup red card interventions. Were you paying attention?

item.title: From giant crab men to searing satire, here is the best TV of 2026 so far
item.description: A mountain of TV has landed in 2026 and we've sifted through it all to deliver you the ones worth your time (or at least a binge-watch).

item.title: The 'stunt' that could cost UK Reform leader Nigel Farage his political career
item.description: Nigel Farage is facing intensifying scrutiny over his finances and millions of dollars' worth of donations. Here's how he got here.

item.title: Telstra recently warned about timing issue linked to national outage
item.description: Government agencies and academics both recently warned Telstra it was vulnerable to the type of error that caused this week's national outage.

item.title: How Beijing 'tripled the size of its nuclear arsenal' in six years
item.description: China's firing of a dummy warhead was the first time in two years it tested its long-range missile arsenal — and the first time ever it had launched such a rocket from a nuclear-powered submarine.

item.title: Climate change becoming a major driver of child marriage across Asia and the Pacific
item.description: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

item.title: AI's opportunities and risks front and centre on Albanese government's agenda
item.description: The government can read the numbers, and one thing they tell us is that without refuelling that vital growth agent, productivity, we will stay in the doldrums.

item.title: AI, gambling and Middle East set for Labor conference debate
item.description: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

item.title: Africa CDC says Ebola outbreak is 'fastest-growing ever' after 600 deaths since mid-May
item.description: New numbers from health agencies show there have been 1,759 confirmed cases in DR Congo since the outbreak was declared.

item.title: France's superstars step up to beat Morocco and reach World Cup semis
item.description: Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé deliver France a comprehensive 2-0 win over Morocco to send Les Bleus into the World Cup semifinals.

item.title: Trump's new Iran threats could mean all-out war but there are other options
item.description: Not only is the war not over, it is once again at a dangerous inflection point, as the US and Iran revert to military options to deal with differences over their interim peace agreement.

item.title: The joy of Neil: How a cheeky seal on Tasmania’s shores brought happiness to the world
item.description: Neil the seal has left his resting spot in Tasmania, leaving a wildly popular social media catalogue of bollard-bashing, fence-crushing videos.

item.title: Queensland campaign to support renters with disability to request home modifications
item.description: About one in three Queenslanders with disability surveyed say they lived in a home that didn't meet their needs last year, a report says.

item.title: AC/DC fans gather to celebrate what would have been Bon Scott's 80th birthday
item.description: AC/DC fans gather in Fremantle to celebrate what would have been the 80th birthday of Bon Scott, who passed away in 1980.

item.title: Aussie Rules club Fregon Bulldogs' new guernseys bring change in fortunes
item.description: The Fregon Bulldogs are among the nation's most remote Australian Rules football clubs, and for several seasons were forced to play in casual clothes. But the club has secured new guernseys — and the change in uniform has led to a change in form.

item.title: Pub, morgue and mine up for lease in historic gold-mining village Hill End
item.description: Sixteen buildings are up for lease in a rare opportunity to shape the future of a tiny community of 110 people on the NSW Central Tablelands.

item.title: Snowy Hydro contractor denies claims it exposed workers to risk of death or injury
item.description: A major contractor on the multi-billion-dollar Snowy Hydro project is defending itself against accusations it exposed five workers to the risk of death or serious injury in two separate incidents.

item.title: Australian War Memorial makes 'magnificent' discovery of first Indigenous Military Cross recipient
item.description: Captain Sidney Leonard Morse's achievements would have remained hidden were it not for a chance discovery by a volunteer historian collating family trees at the Australian War Memorial.

item.title: The meteorite at the centre of a 'culture war'
item.description: The fight to claim a 3.5-tonne space rock didn't just kickstart a publicity battle that divided an empire — it also left a gaping hole in culture. Centuries on, the question remains: Who owned it?

item.title: Environmental groups say Victoria's state forests being logged 'by stealth'
item.description: In the wake of the Victorian government's move away from native timber harvesting, environmental groups are calling for greater management of timber by-product removals in state forests.

item.title: Hanson doubles down on call for Coalition to work with One Nation — as it happened
item.description: Pauline Hanson has doubled down on calls for the Coalition to work with One Nation.

item.title: Workers' pay has not kept pace with productivity growth for 30 years, research suggests
item.description: Research suggests that the typical worker's pay has not kept pace with productivity growth in Australia for 30 years.

item.title: Wally Funk, aviation pioneer and oldest woman to launch into space, dies aged 87
item.description: American aviation pioneer Wally Funk, who was the oldest woman to go to space, has died at 87 in Texas.

item.title: Ilfracombe's Machinery Mile grows as 20th century tractors arrive from state museum
item.description: Ilfracombe's roadside museum is almost as long as the town itself and enshrines machines from Australia's past. Locals are preparing to display the latest donation of four historic tractors.

item.title: ASX closes the week higher after Telstra told to 'face the music' over outage — As it happened
item.description: The Australian share market closed higher on Friday after the latest escalation of tensions in the Middle East sent oil prices higher. Telstra faced further pressure over the national network outage.

item.title: NDIS watchdog slammed for 'snail's pace' response to neglect allegations
item.description: Disability sector workers say lives are being put at risk due to the NDIS watchdog's failure to act on serious safety complaints in one of Australia's remotest regions.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Karolina Muchová and Linda Nosková set up all-Czech final as Coco Gauff misses golden chance
item.description: Coco Gauff is left to rue a regulation miss on match point as Karolina Muchová and Linda Nosková set up an all-Czech date at Wimbledon.

item.title: Tadej Pogačar's daring knows no bounds, crushing Tour de France opposition on Tourmalet
item.description: Tadej Pogačar delivers another masterclass in grand tour dominance, conquering the Col du Tourmalet and crushing his Tour de France rivals with a daring attack.

item.title: Australia lodges formal objection to Trump administration's 'slavery tariff' plan
item.description: The Australian embassy in Washington lodges an objection to the Trump administration's proposal for a 12.5 per cent tariff on Australian imports.

item.title: CFA's high-tech push to prepare for a perilous bushfire season
item.description: From virtual reality training to drones, these tools are helping firefighters prepare for a fast-approaching, potentially severe bushfire season.

item.title: WA wildlife carers lacking resources at frontline of state's bird flu response
item.description: WA wildlife carers say the state government has forgotten them as PPE supplies and veterinary resources run dangerously low.

item.title: How cost of living and changing drinking habits are affecting Hobart's nightlife
item.description: This weekend will be the first since the sudden closure of seven Hobart venues. A manager of another bar says he doesn't know what to expect.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: England defender banned, France's six-minute surge, Mbappé injury scare
item.description: An England defender pays the price for a terrible red-card tackle, and it's the Mbappé show in Boston as France advances. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Indigenous whale-watching tour shares ancient stories of Illawarra coastline
item.description: A new Indigenous-led whale-watching cruise is aiming to give an intimate insight into First Nations people's deep historical connection with the majestic mammals.

item.title: Judge hands down verdict in Jayson Gillham and Melbourne Symphony Orchestra trial
item.description: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

item.title: Cruz Hewitt through to Wimbledon juniors semifinal as Aussie duo falls short in mixed doubles final
item.description: Cruz Hewitt continues to better his famous father's Wimbledon juniors record after yet another convincing win at the All England Club.

item.title: Australia Post worker charged over alleged mail tampering at Perth Airport
item.description: Australia Post worker Hussam Kassem is charged by police with mail tampering at a Perth Airport sorting facility, with $34,000 seized during a search of his home and car.

item.title: Traditional owners say connection to country ignored under flawed legislation
item.description: Traditional owners from WA's Wheatbelt say a historic native title settlement has increased division among Noongar groups.

item.title: Eight US men charged over alleged terror plot to kill Donald Trump at UFC White House event
item.description: Eight men have been accused of planning a drone and shooting attack that aimed to kill the US president and SpaceX founder at the UFC Freedom 250 event held at the White House in June.

item.title: Lexi Minetree on how she embraced an iconic role
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Telstra CEO Vicki Brady 'deeply sorry' for national outage, says investigations are ongoing
item.description: Telstra chief executive Vicki Brady apologises to the company's customers, saying the telco let them down and that investigations into the cause of Wednesday's Telstra outage are continuing.

item.title: Families unite at the scene of a fatal crash on the Bruce Highway.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Man sentenced to six years and five months jail over sex trafficking of Indonesian girl
item.description: A Sydney judge found 45-year-old Surya Subekti has demonstrated no genuine remorse.

item.title: Why potatoes can be the star of the show and what types to use
item.description: Potatoes are undeniably versatile and tasty but choosing which variety to buy has never been more complicated. Brushed or washed? Yellow, white or pink? Mashing or roasting? And now there are low-carb spuds too.

item.title: H5 bird flu strain detected in Australian seabird for first time
item.description: CSIRO lab testing has confirmed the detection of H5 bird flu in a greater crested tern — the first time the strain has been detected in an Australian seabird, after previous confirmed cases involved migratory birds from the sub-Antarctic region.

item.title: Social media giants under fire for refusal to engage with antisemitism royal commission
item.description: Social media giants, including Elon Musk's X Corp, have come under fire with the royal commission on antisemitism expressing frustration at platforms' refusal to engage.

item.title: BBL to play first overseas match in India as Scorchers face Renegades
item.description: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

item.title: Police find human remains during search for missing Victorian teen
item.description: Detectives are investigating after human remains were found during a search for a missing teenage girl in Victoria's west.

item.title: Perth's youngest garbage collector
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Claim woman's death caused by Telstra outage is 'incorrect', SA Police say
item.description: South Australia's police chief says there is "nothing to suggest" the death of a person in a regional hospital was caused by any failure to connect to Triple Zero (000) during the Telstra outage, following an investigation into the matter.

item.title: The Rolling Stones — Foreign Tongues review: a late-career peak in their eighties
item.description: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

item.title: World Cup: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win in any fashion
item.description: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

item.title: Charlie Kirk's family requests evidence against accused killer be shown publicly
item.description: The family of conservative activist Charlie Kirk has pleaded with a judge to allow more public access to evidence presented against the man accused of the assassination.

item.title: Spanish wildfire death toll rises as heatwave continues to wreak havoc
item.description: A fast-moving wildfire in southern Spain kills 12 as a heatwave pushes temperatures up over much of the country.

item.title: Coffee Camp cattle farmer plants 14,000 trees for flood mitigation
item.description: Jeff Imeson has planted around 14,000 trees on his property near Nimbin in a bid to help slow the flow of future floods.

item.title: Australia's most played song on radio this week draws AI criticisms
item.description: A cover of the Madonna hit Like a Prayer is at the top of the charts but criticisms have been levied at the artist behind the cover, Josh Fawaz, over his suspected use of artificial intelligence.

item.title: Paul Okon-Engstler reportedly set to sign with German club FC Köln
item.description: Promising midfielder Paul Okon-Engstler is reportedly headed to Germany, the first of an emerging batch of Socceroos to move to bigger leagues.

item.title: Australian woman accused of illegally voting in US faces jury trial in Louisiana
item.description: A Sydney woman will face trial in the US state of Louisiana after being accused of falsely claiming to be a US citizen and voting illegally in two federal elections.

item.title: Lush native lawn ideas for waterwise, lower-maintenance gardens
item.description: If mowing the lawn is getting you down, it might be time to ditch your exotic buffalo, couch or kikuyu grass and switch to a native alternative.

item.title: Wallabies captain says recent results not good enough ahead of Test match against France
item.description: Close enough will not be good enough for the Wallabies, even with an untried flyhalf and familiar French pack ready to inflict a sixth-straight Test defeat on the hosts in Brisbane.

item.title: Professor Clemente explains insights from spider racing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scientists record spiders' speed and movement.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former Reserve Bank economist urges Australia not to allow money to rule politics
item.description: Former Reserve Bank economist Justin Wolfers says Australia urgently needs to consider the role of money in its politics while examining how US President Donald Trump profits from his position.

item.title: This Place: Boonoo Moodla
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tributes for Derryn Hinch after his death in Melbourne at the age of 82
item.description: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

item.title: Townsville apartment caretaker sentenced to nine years in jail for rape
item.description: Alexander Terry was sentenced to nine years in jail for the rape and sexual assault of four women at a Townsville apartment complex where he was a caretaker.

item.title: Police release CCTV footage and switch search focus as family of Jana Armstrong fears the worst
item.description: Police release CCTV footage and switch search focus as the family of young mother Jana Armstrong, who went missing in Toowoomba, fears the worst.

item.title: The ASX finished trading moderately higher on reports the US and Iran will meet for "technical talks".
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Pies coach says AFL review technology 'not up to scratch' after thriller
item.description: Craig McRae takes aim at the AFL's ARC after Collingwood's thrilling win over North Melbourne.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled recovery of orbital-class rocket
item.description: China has successfully tested an experimental rocket retrieval system, state media reported, in the hope of breaking US dominance in reusable rockets.

item.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket recovery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Not gonna talk about that': Luai dodges press conference after another Tigers loss
item.description: The Tigers lose 32-6 to the Warriors at Campbelltown Stadium, with Jarome Luai skipping the post-match press conference after a week of reports about his future with the team.

item.title: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell in collision involving pedestrian
item.description: Police are investigating the collision which took place on Port Road at Woodville on Friday morning.

item.title: Security precaution led Trump to leave Türkiye on his old Air Force One instead of Qatari-gifted jet, sources say
item.description: Officials reportedly urged the US president to swap out his Qatari-donated luxury jet as a security precaution due to renewed hostilities with Iran.

item.title: Bayeux Tapestry returns to Britain for first time in nearly 1,000 years
item.description: The Bayeux Tapestry has arrived in Britain for the first time in nearly 1,000 years, travelling under police escort during a meticulously choreographed journey from France.

item.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up new art gallery in Bangkok, Thailand
item.description: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

item.title: Cold Chisel saxophonist Andy Bickers dies from cancer, tributes paid to 'incredible musician'
item.description: Cold Chisel saxophone player Andy Bickers has died after "hard fought battle with cancer", the band has announced.

item.title: Iran denies requesting US talks, while Donald Trump says ceasefire is 'over'
item.description: Qatari negotiators meet with officials in Iran to seek de-escalation, as Iran denies requesting talks with the United States following a statement from US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Relentless Sinner downs Djokovic, Zverev ends Fery's dream run to set up blockbuster Wimbledon final
item.description: British wildcard Arthur Fery's bid to make Wimbledon history has ended after he was knocked out by Alexander Zverev, who will face off against reigning champion Jannik Sinner in the final.

item.title: Spain strikes late to seal semi berth after Belgium keeper howler
item.description: Spain will face France in a World Cup semifinal after beating Belgium 2-1 thanks to a late Mikel Merino goal and a goalkeeping error.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Jurassic Park, the Scottish play and Wimbledon fruit
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Cape Fear TV show, starring Amy Adams and Javier Bardem, has distinctly modern menace
item.description: The 1957 book The Executioners has been made into two movies and an iconic episode of The Simpsons. It's now a TV show, starring Amy Adams, Patrick Wilson and Javier Bardem.

item.title: All-star cast set sail in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey
item.description: Christopher Nolan and the cast of the highly anticipated The Odyssey sit down with ABC News Breakfast to talk about the upcoming film.

item.title: Telstra faces outage investigation as expert says legal gaps may let it off
item.description: Telstra faces an outage investigation as an expert says legal gaps may let the telco off the hook.

item.title: The battle for the Blue Zones
item.description: The Blue Zones grew into a global wellness brand aided by stories of legendary old age. But a bitter feud over the science has shaken their foundations.

item.title: BYD backflips and offers full refunds after customers given cars from wrong year
item.description: More than 1,200 customers who bought electric and hybrid cars from Chinese maker BYD will be offered a full refund after the company admitted to selling them vehicles from the wrong year.

item.title: Curbing well-off taxpayers' access to key programs could save billions
item.description: Curbing access to the child care subsidy, parental leave pay, aged care and pensions for some of the most well-off taxpayers would free up about $21 billion a year, a new report says.

item.title: The Interview: Jessica Mauboy wants to share the culture and community of her home
item.description: From singing to her daughter to laughing with her aunties, Jessica Mauboy is all about family. This NAIDOC Week, the star is sharing the music, culture and community she grew up with in a whole new way.

item.title: NATO was a lesson in how the geopolitical landscape has shifted around Trump
item.description: Trump demanded to be the centre of attention. But the more significant news out of NATO was really about everyone else.

item.title: He wasn't perfect, but Derryn Hinch confronted sexual abuse when others turned a blind eye
item.description: Derryn Hinch was not a hero to every survivor of sexual abuse, but in the decades when they lacked allies, he was their fiercest public advocate.

item.title: The World Cup just had one of its hottest matches, in a challenge for the sport
item.description: A 38-degree Celsius evening match has raised fresh questions about how football manages heat in a warming world.

item.title: Bradley John Murdoch tells police he doesn't know where Peter Falconio's body is
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NT Police release new footage of Bradley John Murdoch ahead of 25th anniversary of Falconio mystery
item.description: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final days, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.

item.title: 99-year-old Victorian mechanic Ray Gloster might be on the verge of a world record
item.description: Ray Gloster has been a mechanic for 83 years and counting, and at 99, he might be on the verge of a world record.

item.title: Handmade quilts weave culture into Forster NAIDOC Week baby celebration
item.description: Beautiful Indigenous-themed quilts have been made by a group of women for a NAIDOC Week baby celebration on Worimi country, linking cultures and generations.

item.title: After surviving horrific trauma, one woman is helping refugees in Logan heal together
item.description: Esperance Nyirabarahinyuza survived trauma and losing family. Now, she is helping more than 100 refugee and migrant women in Logan rediscover confidence, community and belonging, one conversation at a time.

item.title: Donald Walker skydives to celebrate his 89th birthday on the Sunshine Coast
item.description: Donald isn't alone in chasing thrills at an older age, with data from SkyDive Australia showing more than 14,000 people over 50 have dived since 2023.

item.title: Donald Walker goes skydiving for his 89th birthday
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Torquay grandmother's 32-hour journey home after Telstra outage caused train shutdown
item.description: A bus trip that should have taken only 13 hours turned into a 32-hour interstate marathon for a Torquay grandmother due to this week's Telstra outage, while there is still no date for when Victorian passengers can claim compensation from V/Line.

item.title: Cruz Hewitt becomes first Australian since Alex de Minaur to reach Wimbledon boys' final
item.description: Cruz Hewitt continues his dominant run at Wimbledon, keeping his straight sets record intact en-route to the boys' final.

item.title: Grey nomads welcome in outback Queensland amid slow start to tourism season
item.description: Grey nomads are giving a much-needed boost to outback Queensland towns over the winter tourism season, but locals say more visitors are needed to fuel economic growth.

item.title: What antisemitism inquiry revealed about social media giants and online 'Jew hatred'
item.description: It was gloves off at the royal commission on antisemitism this week, with Elon Musk's X bearing the brunt of the inquiry's frustrations with obstructive social media giants.

item.title: H5 bird flu poses risk to SA's Coorong critical wetlands
item.description: South Australia's Coorong is a sanctuary for birds and marine life but it is increasingly under threat with the arrival of bird flu in Australia the latest in a rolling barrage of crises.

item.title: Fears workers will never receive entitlement from GFG's liquidated companies
item.description: Jun Samonte has spent 14 years working for the same Whyalla business. He now fears he will never receive the $53,000 he is owed in entitlements, despite already waiting a year.

item.title: Former Sydney high school students lead blockbuster movies Moana and Supergirl
item.description: Some cities have one hometown hero, but Newtown has two making the community proud in a double-feature of blockbuster hits you could watch on the same day.

item.title: Football clubs' ad for contentious raffle
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ACT government signs deal to buy CSIRO's Ginninderra site, plans to build 3,000 new homes
item.description: After being flagged for development over a decade ago, the ACT government has reached an agreement for the sale of land at a former CSIRO agriculture research site to construction of thousands new homes.

item.title: Aboriginal artist combining centuries-old glassblowing technique with millennia-old traditional weaving practice
item.description: Lower Southern Arrernte woman Jenni Kemarre Martiniello creates glass artworks by combining a centuries-old Italian glassmaking technique with millennia-old Indigenous weaving practices.

item.title: Australia has a deficit dilemma, could means-testing wealth be the solution?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tim Merlier takes out Stage 7 of Tour de France as Tadej Pogačar retains yellow jersey
item.description: Tim Merlier excels in the sprint stage of the Tour de France as race leader Tadej Pogačar keeps his closest rivals at bay.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Spain's new clutch king and a Colombian death threat scare
item.description: Spain's new clutch king saves the day, a record-breaking streak is finally broken and a Colombian midfielder receives death threats. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Substandard housing in Beagle Bay
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Living in the 'forgotten' community of Beagle Bay
item.description: Residents of the Indigenous community of Beagle Bay say they feel forgotten by their landlord: the Western Australian government.

item.title: Defending champion Grace Kim and Nelly Korda crash out of Evian Championship
item.description: Grace Kim is joined by a host of Aussies who fail to make the cut in France as world No.1 Nelly Korda also makes a shock exit.

item.title: Broken Hill mother exposed to lead as a child fights same battle with her children
item.description: Baylee Stokes lives with the health impacts of being exposed to lead as a child. Now, she's considering moving away from her hometown to protect her own children.

item.title: Roger Cook's captain's call reveals the extent of his power within WA Labor
item.description: This week, WA Premier Roger Cook stood firm against Labor's powerful factions to get the minister he wanted. Three years in, it shows the power he wields, writes Keane Bourke.

item.title: Derryn Hinch's memorable media moments: from radio wars with prime ministers to Dancing with the Stars
item.description: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

item.title: Polling shows Labor is still sliding in Tasmania, despite facing a 12-year-old minority government
item.description: When Labor recorded its lowest electoral result in over a century last year some may have felt the party had hit rock bottom, at least in terms of polling. That may not be entirely the case, writes Lucy MacDonald.

item.title: Youth advocates' concern as NT children's commissioner quits ahead of child protection changes
item.description: Australia's peak advocate for Aboriginal children has described the circumstances around child protection in the Northern Territory as "dire", following the shock resignation of the Territory's children's commissioner.

item.title: Denmark businesses say shire order to remove 'unsafe' road signs will hurt tourism
item.description: Businesses in a popular West Australian tourist destination say a move by the local shire to ban some roadside signs will cut traffic to their stores.

item.title: Passenger hospitalised after being partially sucked out of window on Ryanair flight from Greece
item.description: The man's head and shoulders were reportedly outside the Boeing 737's window before passengers on the Ryanair flight were able to pull him back in.

item.title: WA Police arrest two men after man's body found on South Terrace, Fremantle
item.description: Police have arrested two men in their 20s after a man was found unresponsive near Fremantle's nightlife precinct overnight.

item.title: Remembering the quadriplegic artist who drew the outback without using his hands
item.description: Left quadriplegic in an accident, every line the late Gunggari man drew and every word he scrawled in ink was created without a pen in his hands.

item.title: Women's shed in isolated Talbingo is now a lifeline of friendship for residents
item.description: In a frosty, isolated town built for long-gone Snowy Hydro Scheme workers, a group of women finds a reason to stay.

item.title: A warming veggie and lentil soup that's ready in less than 30 minutes - ABC News
item.description: This easy, healthy, pantry-based soup is a saviour if your fridge is looking bare.

item.title: Nina Kennedy sets eyes on magical 5m mark after setting new Australian pole vault record
item.description: Nina Kennedy rounds into shape ahead of the Commonwealth Games with a stunning jump in Monte Carlo.

item.title: Donald Trump and Giorgia Meloni's rift laid bare at NATO
item.description: Falling out with Donald Trump is not a situation most world leaders want to find themselves in, but for Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, a spat with the US president could end up playing well for her.

item.title: Hepburn Shire mayor says charges in 'private prosecution' by resident a 'threat to democracy'
item.description: The mayor of Hepburn Shire in Victoria is the third council representative to be charged by the same resident via a rare "private prosecution".

item.title: Details emerge about 16 children who lived in Ohio house of horrors
item.description: From moving around southern Ohio without leaving a paper trail, to being married when they were teenagers, details are emerging about the Siders family.

item.title: World Cup: England captain Harry Kane confirms 'surreal' golf date with US President Donald Trump
item.description: England's captain Harry Kane opens up about his 18-hole golf experience with the US President on a previous visit to Palm Beach, Florida.

item.title: Police investigate after four people found 'unresponsive' in Mount Gambier home
item.description: Police are investigating after four people were found "unresponsive" in a South Australian home earlier this morning.

item.title: Young Indonesians fear for future after startup founder jailed
item.description: The sentencing of tech entrepreneur Nadiem Makarim leaves many young Indonesians questioning whether investing in their country could expose them to legal risks.

item.title: Mark Webber says Oscar Piastri is committed to McLaren F1 amid Red Bull, Max Verstappen rumours
item.description: In the wake of persistent speculation and rumour, Oscar Piastri has no intention of leaving McLaren, his manager has said.

item.title: Live: Saints host Power as WorkSafe Victoria launches investigation into AFL
item.description: St Kilda takes on Port Adelaide at Docklands as the AFL finds itself in hot water over alleged concussion failings. Follow live.

item.title: Jarome Luai to leave Wests Tigers at end of season
item.description: Wests Tigers have confirmed star five-eighth Jarome Luai will leave the club at the end of the season.

item.title: Donald community comes together to mourn death of missing teen Layla Jeffery
item.description: The town of Donald in Victoria's west is in mourning, after remains believed to be missing 13-year-old Layla Jeffery were found by police on Thursday.

item.title: Tasmanian man tied up with cable ties, house burnt down as thieves allegedly steal 9 guns, car
item.description: Emergency services called to a house fire at Tasmania's Coles Bay find the home owner tied up and his home torched after thieves allegedly made off with nine guns and his car.

item.title: 'Chasing an iconic fish': Hundreds of anglers hit remote Daly River for the NT Barra Classic
item.description: Crocs, sharks and an Australian cricket legend have all turned up for one of Australia's biggest barra fishing tournaments on the Daly River.

item.title: Meta pulls new Instagram AI feature after widespread backlash
item.description: The automatic feature, integrated into its Meta AI chatbot, let users edit generated images directly through sketches.
